briefdescription.para: When the animation ends, the animated property values are saved. @SINCE_1_0.0.
When the animation ends, the animated property values are forgotten. @SINCE_1_0.0.
If the animation is stopped, the animated property values are saved as if the animation had run to completion, otherwise behaves like Bake. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Enumeration for what to do when the animation ends, is stopped, or is destroyed. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Values in between key frames are interpolated using a linear polynomial. (Default) @SINCE_1_0.0.
Values in between key frames are interpolated using a cubic polynomial. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Enumeration for what interpolation method to use on key-frame animations. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Animation has stopped @SINCE_1_1.21.
The animation is playing @SINCE_1_1.21.
The animation is paused @SINCE_1_1.21.
Enumeration for what state the animation is in.

detaileddescription.para: Note: Calling Reset() on this class will NOT reset the animation. It will call BaseHandle::Reset() which drops the object handle.

briefdescription.para: When the animation arrives at the end in looping mode, the animation restarts from the beginning. @SINCE_1_2.60.
When the animation arrives at the end in looping mode, the animation reverses direction and runs backwards again. @SINCE_1_2.60.
Enumeration for what looping mode is in.
Animation finished signal type @SINCE_1_0.0.
Interpolation function @SINCE_1_0.0.
Creates an uninitialized Animation; this can be initialized with Animation::New().

detaileddescription.para: Calling member functions with an uninitialized Animation handle is not allowed. @SINCE_1_0.0

briefdescription.para: Destructor.

detaileddescription.para: This is non-virtual since derived Handle types must not contain data or virtual methods. @SINCE_1_0.0

briefdescription.para: This copy constructor is required for (smart) pointer semantics.
This assignment operator is required for (smart) pointer semantics.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
rhs
A reference to the copied handle
A reference to this

briefdescription.para: Move constructor.
Move assignment operator.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_9.22
rhs
A reference to the moved handle
A reference to this handle

briefdescription.para: Sets the duration of an animation.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
seconds
The duration in seconds
DurationSeconds must be greater than zero.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the duration of an animation.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
The duration in seconds

briefdescription.para: Sets whether the animation will loop.

detaileddescription.para: This function resets the loop count and should not be used with SetLoopCount(int). Setting this parameter does not cause the animation to Play().

briefdescription.para: Enables looping for 'count' repeats.

detaileddescription.para: A zero is the same as SetLooping(true) i.e. repeat forever. This function resets the looping value and should not be used with SetLooping(bool). Setting this parameter does not cause the animation to Play().

briefdescription.para: Gets the loop count.

detaileddescription.para: A zero is the same as SetLooping(true) ie repeat forever. The loop count is initially 1 for play once.

@SINCE_1_1.20
The number of times to loop

briefdescription.para: Gets the current loop count.

detaileddescription.para: A value 0 to GetLoopCount() indicating the current loop count when looping.

@SINCE_1_1.20
The current number of loops that have occured

briefdescription.para: Queries whether the animation will loop.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
True if the animation will loop

briefdescription.para: Sets the end action of the animation.

detaileddescription.para:
This action is performed when the animation ends or if it is stopped. Default end action is bake. @SINCE_1_0.0
action
The end action

briefdescription.para: Returns the end action of the animation.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
The end action

briefdescription.para: Sets the disconnect action.

detaileddescription.para:
If any of the animated property owners are disconnected from the stage while the animation is being played, then this action is performed. Default action is to BakeFinal. @SINCE_1_0.0
disconnectAction
The disconnect action

briefdescription.para: Returns the disconnect action.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
The disconnect action

briefdescription.para: Sets the default alpha function for an animation.

detaileddescription.para:
This is applied to individual property animations, if no further alpha functions are supplied. @SINCE_1_0.0
alpha
The default alpha function

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the default alpha function for an animation.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
The default alpha function

briefdescription.para: Sets the progress of the animation.

detaileddescription.para: The animation will play (or continue playing) from this point. The progress must be in the 0-1 interval or in the play range interval if defined ( See Animation::SetPlayRange ), otherwise, it will be ignored.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the current progress of the animation.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
The current progress as a normalized value between [0,1]

briefdescription.para: Specifies a speed factor for the animation.

detaileddescription.para: The speed factor is a multiplier of the normal velocity of the animation. Values between [0,1] will slow down the animation and values above one will speed up the animation. It is also possible to specify a negative multiplier to play the animation in reverse.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the speed factor of the animation.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
Speed factor

briefdescription.para: Sets the playing range.

detaileddescription.para: Animation will play between the values specified. Both values ( range.x and range.y ) should be between 0-1, otherwise they will be ignored. If the range provided is not in proper order ( minimum,maximum ), it will be reordered.

briefdescription.para: Gets the playing range.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
The play range defined for the animation

briefdescription.para: Sets the blend point to interpolate animate property.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_2_2.26
blendPoint
A value between [0,1], If the value of the keyframe whose progress is 0 is different from the current value, the property is animated as it smoothly blends until the progress reaches the blendPoint.
The blend point only affects animation registered with AnimateBetween. Other animations operate the same as when Play() is called. And the blend point needs to be set before this animation plays. If the blend point changes after playback, animation continuity cannot be guaranteed.
In the case of a looping animation, the animation is blended only in the first loop.

briefdescription.para: Gets the Blend Point.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_2_2.26
The blend point to interpolate animate property

briefdescription.para: Play the animation. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Plays the animation from a given point.

detaileddescription.para: The progress must be in the 0-1 interval or in the play range interval if defined ( See Animation::SetPlayRange ), otherwise, it will be ignored.

briefdescription.para: Play the animation after a given delay time.

detaileddescription.para:
The delay time is not included in the looping time. When the delay time is negative value, it would treat as play immediately. @SINCE_1_2.60
delaySeconds
The delay time

briefdescription.para: Pauses the animation. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Queries the state of the animation. @SINCE_1_1.21.
Stops the animation. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Clears the animation.

detaileddescription.para: This disconnects any objects that were being animated, effectively stopping the animation. @SINCE_1_0.0

briefdescription.para: Sets the looping mode.

detaileddescription.para:
Animation plays forwards and then restarts from the beginning or runs backwards again. @SINCE_1_2.60
loopingMode
The looping mode is one of RESTART and AUTO_REVERSE

briefdescription.para: Gets one of the current looping mode.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_2.60
The current looping mode

briefdescription.para: Get the unique id of Animation. It could be 0 given animation is invalid.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_2_3.14
The unique id of Animation, or 0 if invalid.

briefdescription.para: Connects to this signal to be notified when an Animation's animations have finished.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
A signal object to connect with

briefdescription.para: Animates a property value by a relative amount.

detaileddescription.para:
The default alpha function will be used. The effect will start & end when the animation begins & ends. @SINCE_1_0.0
target
The target object/property to animate
relativeValue
The property value will change by this amount

briefdescription.para: Animates a property value by a relative amount.

detaileddescription.para:
The effect will start & end when the animation begins & ends. @SINCE_1_0.0
target
The target object/property to animate
relativeValue
The property value will change by this amount
alpha
The alpha function to apply

briefdescription.para: Animates a property value by a relative amount.

detaileddescription.para:
The default alpha function will be used. @SINCE_1_0.0
target
The target object/property to animate
relativeValue
The property value will increase/decrease by this amount
period
The effect will occur during this time period

memberdef.argsstring: (Property target, Property::Value relativeValue, AlphaFunction alpha, TimePeriod period)
memberdef.name: AnimateBy

briefdescription.para: Animates a property value by a relative amount.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
target
The target object/property to animate
relativeValue
The property value will increase/decrease by this amount
alpha
The alpha function to apply
period
The effect will occur during this time period

briefdescription.para: Animates a property to a destination value.

detaileddescription.para:
The default alpha function will be used. The effect will start & end when the animation begins & ends. @SINCE_1_0.0
target
The target object/property to animate
destinationValue
The destination value

briefdescription.para: Animates a property to a destination value.

detaileddescription.para:
The effect will start & end when the animation begins & ends. @SINCE_1_0.0
target

destinationValue

alpha

detaileddescription.para:
The default alpha function will be used. @SINCE_1_0.0

destinationValue

period

detaileddescription.para:

The target object property to animate
keyFrames
The set of time/value pairs between which to animate
interpolation

detaileddescription.para:

target

keyFrames

alpha

interpolation

briefdescription.para: Animates an actor's position and orientation through a predefined path.

detaileddescription.para: The actor will rotate to orient the supplied forward vector with the path's tangent. If forward is the zero vector then no rotation will happen.

@SINCE_1_0.0
actor
The actor to animate
path
The path. It defines position and orientation
forward
The vector (in local space coordinate system) that will be oriented with the path's tangent direction

memberdef.argsstring: (Actor actor, Path path, const Vector3 &forward, AlphaFunction alpha)
memberdef.name: Animate

briefdescription.para: Animates an actor's position and orientation through a predefined path.

detaileddescription.para: The actor will rotate to orient the supplied forward vector with the path's tangent. If forward is the zero vector then no rotation will happen.

@SINCE_1_0.0
actor
The actor to animate
path
The path. It defines position and orientation
forward
The vector (in local space coordinate system) that will be oriented with the path's tangent direction
alpha
The alpha function to apply

memberdef.argsstring: (Actor actor, Path path, const Vector3 &forward, TimePeriod period)
memberdef.name: Animate

briefdescription.para: Animates an actor's position and orientation through a predefined path.

detaileddescription.para: The actor will rotate to orient the supplied forward vector with the path's tangent. If forward is the zero vector then no rotation will happen.

path

forward

period

briefdescription.para: Animates an actor's position and orientation through a predefined path.

forward
The vector (in local space coordinate system) that will be oriented with the path's tangent direction
alpha

period
The effect will occur during this time period

briefdescription.para: Shows an actor during the animation.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
actor
The actor to animate
delaySeconds
The initial delay from the start of the animation

briefdescription.para: Hides an actor during the animation.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
actor
The actor to animate
delaySeconds
The initial delay from the start of the animation

briefdescription.para: Creates an initialized Animation.

detaileddescription.para:
The animation will not loop. The default end action is "Bake". The default alpha function is linear. @SINCE_1_0.0
durationSeconds
The duration in seconds
A handle to a newly allocated Dali resource
durationSeconds can not be negative.

briefdescription.para: Creates an initialized Animation.

detaileddescription.para:
The animation will not loop. The default end action is "Bake". The default alpha function is linear. @SINCE_2_5.18
A handle to a newly allocated Dali resource

briefdescription.para: Downcasts a handle to Animation handle.

detaileddescription.para: If handle points to an Animation object, the downcast produces valid handle. If not, the returned handle is left uninitialized.

@SINCE_1_0.0
handle
Handle to an object
Handle to an Animation object or an uninitialized handle

briefdescription.para: Dali::Animation can be used to animate the properties of any number of objects, typically Actors.

detaileddescription.para: An example animation setup is shown below:
Dali::Animation supports "fire and forget" behaviour i.e. an animation continues to play if the handle is discarded. Note that in the following example, the "Finish" signal will be emitted:

If the "Finish" signal is connected to a member function of an object, it must be disconnected before the object is destroyed. This is typically done in the object destructor, and requires either the Dali::Connection object or Dali::Animation handle to be stored.

The overall animation time is superseded by the values given in the TimePeriod structure used when calling the AnimateTo(), AnimateBy(), AnimateBetween() and Animate() methods. If any of the individual calls to those functions exceeds the overall animation time, then the overall animation time is automatically extended.
Using AnimateTo and AnimateBy for the same property of the same Actor will yield undefined behaviour especially if the TimePeriod overlaps.
After calling Animation::Play(), Handle::GetProperty will return the target value of the animated property.

Signals
Signal Name
Method
finished
FinishedSignal()

Actions
Action Name
Animation method called
play
Play()
stop
Stop()
pause
Pause()
@SINCE_1_0.0
